title: heliophila pusilla subsp. macrosperma
intro: Heliophila pusilla subsp. macrosperma, also known as Dwarf Namaqualand button or small-flowered buttonflower, is a low-growing, annual plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Heliophila pusilla subsp. macrosperma include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be made into a tea or infused in oil to create a topical treatment for skin issues and other health concerns.
